name: Red Cooked Eggs #2
recipeInstructions:
1. Place eggs in a medium saucepan; cover with cold water.
2. Heat over medium heat to boiling.
3. Reduce heat to low; simmer, uncovered, about 15 minutes.
4. Remove from heat; run cold water into pan until cool. Let eggs cool in cold water. Drain.
5. Lightly tap shells all over with back of spoon; do not crack so hard that shells actually come loose.
6. Return eggs to saucepan; add remaining ingredients.
7. Add cold water to cover egg; heat over medium heat to boiling.
8. Reduce heat to low; simmer, covered, about 1-1/2 hours. Turn off heat at any time and resume cooking later if needed.
9. Remove from heat; let cool to room temperature in cooking liquid.
10. Refrigerate eggs, in cooking liquid, until serving time.
11. At serving time, drain eggs, discarding liquid; shell eggs and leave whole or cut in half. The longer the eggs soak, at least several hours, the better flavor they will have. They can be prepared several days in advance.
